General Settings

The General Settings page (/settings/general) covers three settings groups: company details, currency and locale, and the financial year start month. These settings affect how Merlin presents your organisation on invoices and how it calculates reporting periods.

Company details

Company details are drawn from the CompanySettings group and appear on generated PDF sales invoices and in outbound email footers where applicable. Fill in all fields that should appear on your invoices:

FieldUsed for
NameCompany trading name shown on invoice headers
Address line 1Street address on invoices
Address line 2Suite or unit number; shown below address line 1
CityCity on invoices
State / ProvinceState or province on invoices
Postal codePostal or ZIP code on invoices
CountryCountry on invoices
PhoneContact phone number shown on invoices
EmailContact email shown on invoices
Tax numberVAT, GST, or tax registration number shown on invoices
LogoCompany logo shown on PDF invoices

Currency and locale

The CurrencySettings group controls how money values are formatted and what currency is treated as the home currency:

SettingDefaultNotes
Base currencyZARISO 4217 currency code for your home currency. Foreign-currency invoices are converted to this currency using exchange rates.
Localeen_ZALocale code used for number formatting (e.g. decimal separators, thousands separators). Merlin uses tabular figures for money amounts so columns align in tables and reports.

Financial year

The AccountingSettings group holds a single setting: financial_year_start_month. This is an integer from 1 (January) to 12 (December) representing the first month of your financial year. Merlin uses this to calculate the current financial year's start and end dates, which become the default date range in all reports. NEEDS CONFIRMATION: whether this setting has a default value or must be set explicitly on first installation.

Permissions

NEEDS CONFIRMATION: which permission gates access to the /settings/general page.
